audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Timothy text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for enslaved/masters, healthy words, contentment, love of money, fight of trust, rich warned, final charge.

verses.restored: As many as are under yoke as enslaved ones, let them consider their own masters worthy of all honor, so that the name of the Divine and the teaching may not be blasphemed.

But those having trusting masters, let them not despise them because they are siblings, but rather serve, because those receiving the good service are trusting and beloved. Teach and appeal these things.
If anyone teaches differently and does not come to healthy words, those of our Lord Jesus Messiah, and to the teaching according to reverence/godliness,
one has been puffed up, understanding nothing, but being diseased around disputes and word-battles, from which come envy, strife, slanders, harmful suspicions,
constant friction of humans corrupted in mind and deprived of the truth, supposing reverence/godliness to be means of gain.
But reverence/godliness with contentment is great gain.
For we brought nothing into the world, neither are we able to carry anything out.
But having food and coverings, with these we will be content.
But those wishing to be rich fall into testing and snare and many foolish and harmful desires, which sink humans into ruin and destruction.
For root of all kinds of harmful things is love of silver/money, which some reaching after were led astray from the trust and pierced themselves around with many pains.
But you, human of Divine, flee these things; pursue righteousness/justice, reverence/godliness, trust, love, endurance, gentleness.
Struggle the beautiful/good struggle of the trust; take hold of the age-long life to which you were called and confessed the beautiful/good confession before many witnesses.
I charge you before the Divine, the One making all things alive, and Messiah Jesus, the One testifying the beautiful/good confession before Pontius Pilate,
to keep the command spotless, irreproachable, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Messiah,
which in its own seasons the blessed and only Sovereign will show, the King of those reigning and Lord of those lording,
the only One having deathlessness, dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no human has seen nor is able to see; to whom honor and age-long might. Amen.
Command those rich in the present age not to be high-minded, nor to hope upon uncertainty of riches, but upon the Divine, the One richly providing us all things for enjoyment,
to do good, to be rich in beautiful/good works, to be generous, ready to share,
storing up for themselves a beautiful/good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of true life.
O Timothy, guard the deposit, turning away from profane empty-sounds and oppositions of falsely named knowledge,
which some professing have missed the mark concerning the trust. Grace be with you.

safety_note: 1 Timothy 6:1-2 preserves ancient slavery instructions but must not be used to sanctify slavery or coercive labor. Read with the broader canonical trajectory of release, siblinghood, justice, and the dignity of enslaved people. Wealth warnings should not shame survival needs; they critique greed, status, and trust in riches.
